findings.title: Rate-limit guard disabled — security check neutralized
findings.evidence: Guard condition `if (overLimit(req.ip))` replaced with `if (0)`, which is always falsy. The `rejectRequest(req)` call inside the block will never execute, completely disabling rate-limit enforcement. This is a critical security bypass that prevents the application from rejecting requests that exceed the rate limit.

notes: Diff Drift severity marked as 'low', but the actual impact is high: this is an active security control removal that directly allows rate-limit bypass attacks. Conservative severity escalation from low to high is justified by the concrete evidence of disabled security enforcement.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 multi-model panel; blind, packet-only context.
